name: Kardigan, Inc.
business: Kardigan is a clinical-stage precision therapeutics company developing medicines that target the root cause of specific cardiovascular diseases where no approved treatments exist. It has three late-stage programs: Danicamtiv for genetic dilated cardiomyopathy, Ataciguat for moderate calcific aortic valve stenosis, and Tonlamarsen for acute severe hypertension post-hospitalization.
sellsTo: Patients with moderate calcific aortic valve stenosis
Patients with acute severe hypertension post-hospitalization

exposures.sentence: We have three late-stage programs, Danicamtiv, Ataciguat, and Tonlamarsen, in clinical development for indications for which no approved therapies currently exist.
Our Prolaio technology platform is currently integrated across each of our clinical trials and utilized by all participating patients.

By integrating third-party clinical-grade wearable sensors, a patient-friendly user interface, and proprietary Class I and Class II FDA-regulated devices, including 510(k)-cleared algorithms, Prolaio enables continuous physiologic data collection and customized patient-reported outcome information from patients in their daily lives outside of the clinic.

catalysts.event: Topline data from Phase 2b portion of KINSHIP-DCM for Danicamtiv
catalysts.sentence: We anticipate sharing topline data from the Phase 2b portion of the KINSHIP-DCM in the first half of 2027.
We anticipate sharing 24-week data in the first half of 2027 and 48-week data in the second half of 2027 from KATALYST-AV.
We anticipate sharing 24-week data in the first half of 2027 and 48-week data in the second half of 2027 from KATALYST-AV.

catalysts.event: Topline data from Phase 2b portion of KARDINAL-ASH for Tonlamarsen
catalysts.sentence: We anticipate sharing topline data from the Phase 2b portion of KARDINAL-ASH in the first half of 2027.

tamClaims.claim: DCM affects up to 150,000-200,000 U.S. patients with diagnosed primary genetic or familial DCM.
tamClaims.sentence: DCM is the most common cardiomyopathy in adults with primary genetic DCM, affecting up to between 150,000 and 200,000 U.S. patients with diagnosed primary genetic or familial DCM.

tamClaims.claim: There are 40,000-50,000 patients with sarcomeric variants (MYH7 or TTN).
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate there are 40,000 to 50,000 patients with sarcomeric variants (majority being MYH7 or TTN variants).

tamClaims.claim: CAVS affects 3.3 million patients in the United States.
tamClaims.sentence: CAVS is the third most common cardiovascular disease after HTN and coronary artery disease, affecting 3.3 million patients in the United States alone.

tamClaims.claim: Moderate CAVS affects approximately one million U.S. patients.
tamClaims.sentence: Moderate CAVS is a progressive disease affecting approximately one million U.S. patients.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately six million ASH patients present in emergency rooms and about two million cases lead to hospitalization each year in the United States.
tamClaims.sentence: Approximately six million ASH patients present in emergency rooms and about two million cases leading to hospitalization each year in the United States.

tamClaims.claim: The total cost of heart failure in the United States is projected to reach approximately $70 billion by 2030.
tamClaims.sentence: The total cost of heart failure in the United States is projected to reach approximately $70 billion by 2030.

tamClaims.claim: Precision cardiovascular therapeutics for HCM and transthyretin amyloidosis represent a potential global opportunity between $8 billion and $19 billion.
tamClaims.sentence: The development of precision cardiovascular therapeutics, particularly targeted for the treatments of hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (HCM) and transthyretin amyloidosis, represents a potential global opportunity between $8 billion and $19 billion.
